meaning: adj.未入睡的,警觉的,时刻戒备的
examples: It's so tired that they are unsleeping all night.
I have always watched her performances, it kept me busy through unsleeping nights, and it gave me sweet dreams during the day.
Or are both sleeping and waking, dreaming and acting, informed by an unsleeping and ever-living consciousness that merely and utterly is?
Indeed, the unsleeping doctor would have had to go all the way back to Galen, the ancient Roman physician whose teachings had dominated medicine until Galileo had challenged him a century before.
